phrasalVerbs.definition: To move things so that they are in different places or positions.
examples: She changed around the furniture to create more space.
Let's change around the seating arrangement for the meeting.
They changed around the office layout to improve workflow.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To repair, paint, and improve an old building, car, boat, etc.
examples: They're doing up an old farmhouse in the countryside.
He did up a vintage car and sold it for a profit.
The restaurant was recently done up with a modern interior.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To clean, repair, or decorate something.
examples: We need to fix up the spare room before guests arrive.
She fixed up the garden beautifully for the party.
The community fixed up the playground for the children.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To change someone or something so that they become something else.
examples: The warehouse was made into luxury apartments.
She made the old dress into a fashionable new outfit.
The novel was made into a successful film.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To change or improve the appearance of someone or something.
examples: The stylists made over the participants on the TV show.
They made over the company's image with new branding.
The garden was completely made over in just one weekend.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To make a surface look better with small improvements.
examples: The photograph was touched up to remove imperfections.
He touched up the paintwork before putting the house on sale.
She touched up her makeup before the presentation.

fillInExercises.sentence: We ___ the living room furniture to make it feel different.
They spent years ___ up the Victorian house.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Do up' means to renovate or improve a property.

fillInExercises.sentence: Volunteers ___ the community centre before the opening.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Fix up' means to repair and improve something.

fillInExercises.sentence: The factory was ___ into a trendy art gallery.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Make into' means to transform something into something else.

fillInExercises.sentence: The TV show ___ ordinary people into stunning celebrities.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Make over' means to transform someone's appearance.

fillInExercises.sentence: Just ___ up the scratches on the car before the inspection.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Touch up' means to make minor improvements.

fillInExercises.sentence: The old barn was ___ a charming wedding venue.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Make into' means to convert into something different.

fillInExercises.sentence: They're planning to ___ over the company headquarters.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Make over' means to renovate or completely change the appearance.

lines.speaker: Oliver
lines.text: We've decided to [do up|do up] the house before selling it.

lines.speaker: Clara
lines.text: Good idea. Will you [change around|change around] the layout as well?

lines.speaker: Oliver
lines.text: Yes, and we'll [fix up|fix up] the garden too.

lines.speaker: Clara
lines.text: The garage could be [made into|make into] an extra bedroom.

lines.speaker: Oliver
lines.text: That would really [make over|make over] the whole property.

lines.speaker: Clara
lines.text: Just [touch up|touch up] the paintwork and it'll look perfect.

lines.speaker: Emma
lines.text: Management wants to [make over|make over] the entire office floor.

lines.speaker: James
lines.text: Are they going to [change around|change around] the desks?

lines.speaker: Emma
lines.text: Yes, and the storage room will be [make into|make into] a meeting space.

lines.speaker: James
lines.text: Someone needs to [fix up|fix up] the kitchen area too.

lines.speaker: Emma
lines.text: They're [doing up|do up] the reception as the first priority.

lines.speaker: James
lines.text: I'll [touch up|touch up] my desk area before the big reveal.
